body:
![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The `useRef` is initialized with `null`, but its generic type `ComponentBinding<T>` does not include `null`. With strict null checks enabled in your `tsconfig.json`, this will cause a type error. The ref's type should be explicitly set to `ComponentBinding<T> | null` to reflect its possible null value.

```suggestion
    const bindingRef = useRef<ComponentBinding<T> | null>(null);
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

`rxjs` and `zod` are used by the library but are not listed as `peerDependencies`. Based on the installation instructions in `README.md` and the externals in `vite.config.ts`, they should be declared as `peerDependencies` to ensure that the consuming application provides compatible versions.

```json
  "peerDependencies": {
    "react": "^19.2.0",
    "react-dom": "^19.2.0",
    "rxjs": "^7.8.1",
    "zod": "^3.23.8"
  }
```
Should we do this in a separate, parallel job?
nth-of-type seems pretty brittle, could you apply a class to this or something instead?
These are pretty tonally different fonts, with two different reasonable fallbacks...do we really need all of these?

I think you're going to want to memoize these for performance, but I think if you do so, you'll need to provide a custom equality function since ReactWrapper() is dynamically generated and will always fail an equality test during memoization.
Does it make sense to prefix all of these with "React"? It's already in a directory called react/ using React's `tsx` files.
Hmm, usually a JS/TS compiler is smart enough to read the JSON itself to produce a schema. Is this needed?

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The setup instructions in this README appear to contain outdated paths and package names from a previous prototype, which will prevent users from successfully building and running the explorer app.

- The dependency should be `@a2ui/react` (in `renderers/react`), not `@a2ui/react_prototype`.
- The `cd` command in "Building Dependencies" (line 17) should likely be `cd ../..` to navigate from `a2ui_explorer` to the `react` package root, or `cd renderers/react` if run from the repository root.
- The `cd` command in "Setup and Development" (line 32) points to `samples/client/react`, but this explorer app is at `renderers/react/a2ui_explorer`.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The `ex` parameter is typed as `any`. Given the known structure of the example data, we can use a more specific type to improve type safety and code clarity.

```suggestion
const getMessages = (ex: { messages: unknown[] } | unknown[] | undefined) => (Array.isArray(ex) ? ex : ex?.messages);
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

To improve type safety and avoid using `any`, let's specify the `ReactComponentImplementation` type for the message processor state. This will also allow removing a type assertion on line 81.

```suggestion
  const [processor, setProcessor] = useState<MessageProcessor<ReactComponentImplementation> | null>(null);
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

Using `toLocaleTimeString()` for log timestamps can lead to inconsistencies across different user environments and makes parsing logs harder. It's better to use a standardized format like ISO 8601.

```suggestion
          setLogs((l) => [...l, {time: new Date().toISOString(), action}]);
```
